mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of observations where FEDFUNDS rises month-over-month, contradicting the negative direction prediction.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market signals an imminent economic contraction, which reduces aggregate consumer demand and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 generates positive 20-day forward returns in over 50% of instances where the unemployment rate increases by more than 0.5 percentage points within a three-month window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in residential fixed investment (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates a severe housing sector shock that drags down aggregate demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST does not predict negative 60-day forward returns in more than 40% of historical instances, or the combined signal yields a Rank IC below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-shock recovery phase, rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects resilient underlying demand and capacity utilization, which mechanically boosts short-term equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over five trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO rises consecutively for three months while the S&P 500 delivers negative 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of those periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 320, persistent inflation pressures force the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, which compresses equity risk premiums and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL remains above 320 for consecutive months while the S&P 500 posts positive 20-day forward returns in over 55% of those months.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP (GDPC1) and total nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) confirms a broad-based economic expansion that supports sustained corporate profit growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Both GDPC1 and PAYEMS show positive month-over-month growth while the S&P 500 generates negative 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of overlapping instances.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trend in the unemployment rate reflects weakening labor demand, which mechanically reduces consumer spending and depresses short-term equity valuations, yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over five trading days.
failure_condition: The unemployment rate trends upward for three consecutive months while the S&P 500 delivers positive 5-day forward returns in more than 65% of those periods.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 1300 thousand units, the housing sector drag on GDP accelerates, leading to negative equity returns over the next month as construction-related supply chains contract and consumer wealth declines.
failure_condition: HOUST remains below 1300 thousand units for two consecutive months while the S&P 500 posts positive 20-day forward returns in over 50% of those instances.
